canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, SIB-200 is consistently presented as a measurement instrument for text classification. It is defined as a multilingual benchmark that spans 204 languages and multiple domains. Papers use SIB-200 to evaluate models on the sentence-level semantic task of topic classification, sometimes in a zero-shot setting. The stated evaluation goals are to assess both "classification accuracy" and "cross-domain generalization." One paper describes an operationalization of the latter, where models are trained on one domain and then evaluated on all other domains within the benchmark. The data also indicates that specific language subsets, such as Filipino and Cebuano, are sometimes used for evaluation.
papers: AbsVis – Benchmarking How Humans and Vision-Language Models “See” Abstract Concepts in Images
Empowering Math Problem Generation and Reasoning for Large Language Model via Synthetic Data based Continual Learning Framework
How DoesDPOReduce Toxicity? A Mechanistic Neuron-Level Analysis
Where to show Demos in Your Prompt: A Positional Bias of In-Context Learning

definitions.text: Multilingual text classification benchmark spanning 204 languages and multiple domains, used to evaluate cross-domain generalization and classification accuracy.

relationships.evidence: We also take the Filipino and Cebuano subsets of SIB-200 (Adelani et al., 2024), and the text-only subset of Balita NLP (Buñag and Esquivel, 2023). (Section 3.2)
